images.altText: A person walks past a damaged building
People walk across the flattened remains of a bombed out apartment building with rubble strewn in all directions.
Emergency workers sitting on top of a truck parked by tall apartment towers partially destroyed by an air strike.

articles.title: Trump's threat against 'a whole civilisation' is coming back to haunt him

children.content: Donald Trump has survived what may have brought down many presidents before him. But the "end of civilisation" threat, as it's become known, has exposed a vulnerability he has not faced before.

image.altText: A close up of Donald Trump mid speech.

articles.title: Trump criticises NATO over Iran in meeting with alliance's boss

children.content: US President Donald Trump vents his frustration with NATO during a private meeting with its secretary-general as relations in the military alliance reach a crisis point over the Iran war.

image.altText: A close up of Mark Rutte, who wears a suit and glasses, against a blue background

articles.title: As Trump's rhetoric escalates, Albanese diplomatically eyes off-ramp

children.content: Neither side of politics in Australia rushed to enthusiastically celebrate the ceasefire deal that followed US President Donald Trump's threat.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands before a flag.

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

image.altText: Explosion in a city.
Smoke rises over a city skyline
Fire trucks surround the rubble of a building in the dark

children.content: Donald Trump says if an agreement is not reached, the "shootin' starts bigger and better and stronger than anyone has ever seen before". Follow live.

items.title: 'Bigger, better and stronger' attacks in Middle East threatened by Trump unless 'real agreement' is reached

updates.title: Netanyahu says strikes will continue on Hezbeollah 'wherever necessary'
News agencies say Iran put sea mines in Strait of Hormuz
Israeli military says it has killed senior Hezbollah official

items.title: Warships 'could absolutely deploy' to Iran if asked, defence chief says

children.content: Australia's chief of defence says Australia could "absolutely" deploy a warship to the Strait of Hormuz if asked by the federal government.

image.altText: A clean-shaven man with glasses and a military uniform shirt sits pensively behind a microphone.

items.title: The Iran war has become a cash cow for the Kremlin

children.content: Donald Trump used to love using the word "winning" but out of the wreckage of the Iran war and the world's oil supplies slowing, there is one man truly winning: Russia's Vladimir Putin.

image.altText: Vladimir Putin gestures while speaking at a white lectern.

items.title: Gang rapist on the run after escaping custody at Sydney hospital

children.content: A man convicted of gang raping a 14-year-old girl in a Western Sydney park has escaped from custody while at a hospital. Follow live.

image.altText: A composite image of a man's face and the same man running across a road.

items.title: High Court refuses to hear Bruce Lehrmann's appeal

children.content: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that found, on the balance of probabilities, he did rape Brittany Higgins.

image.altText: Bruce walks outside, flanked by others, wearing a blue suit, white shirt and pink tie and glasses.

items.title: People living in vehicles forced to choose between fuel and food

children.content: The high cost of fuel is making it even more difficult for people living in their cars to afford food, electricity and transport.

articles.title: Diesel prices hit new post-war peak, despite excise cuts

children.content: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

articles.title: How long will it take petrol prices to get back to pre-Iran war levels, if at all?

children.content: Iran's promise to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after striking a temporary agreement with the US saw the cost of oil fall sharply. But how quickly are we likely to see relief at the bowser in Australia? Analysts say it could be weeks or months.

image.altText: Fuel bowser with a big truck in the background.

articles.title: How is the war in Iran impacting you or your business? Share with us

children.content: As fuel costs soar and the global economy reacts to a shortage of oil supplies, we need your help in identifying the impacts here in Australia. Whether it's the cost of your work commute or supply shortages affecting your business, share it with us.

image.altText: A row of petrol pumps.

gallery.altText: Anthony sits in a camp chair and reads a book outside his van.
Anthony sits in a camp chair outside his van. He has a cap, a beard and a worried expression.

thumbnail.altText: Anthony sits in a camp chair and reads a book outside his van.
Anthony sits in a camp chair outside his van. He has a cap, a beard and a worried expression.

avatar.alt: A headshot of ABC's Tony Ibrahim

items.title: Festival of footy kicks off with Crows–Blues sellout in Adelaide

children.content: The fourth edition of AFL's Gather Round gets underway with the Adelaide Crows hosting Carlton at a packed Adelaide Oval. Follow live.

image.altText: The Adelaide Crows men's AFL team run out through a Gather Round banner at Adelaide Oval.

items.title: Perfect Panthers look to continue their unbeaten start against Bulldogs

children.content: The Penrith Panthers are unbeaten to start the NRL season, looking to extend that run against the Bulldogs at Stadium Australia. Follow live.

image.altText: Two rugby league players in pink hug and celebrate a try with players in pink and players in red and blue in front

items.title: Canavan's rebrand and new Facebook photo, Albanese's Trump rebuke

children.content: With retro shades, big rhetoric and a rejection of net zero, Matt Canavan is hoping to reshape the Nationals. His first big test is just weeks away.

image.altText: Matt Canavan

items.title: Grace Tame Foundation announces closure

children.content: The Grace Tame Foundation, established by the Tasmanian-born survivor of child sexual abuse, has announced it is closing because securing funding is becoming "increasingly challenging".

image.altText: A woman wearing a grey cardigan with her hair in a pony tail looks seriously at the camera in a building corridor.

items.title: King Charles writes 'very different' open letter after NT flood disaster

children.content: King Charles has written an open letter to Northern Territory residents following recent devastating flooding, saying the "increasingly catastrophic effects of climate change are surely a siren-call to the world for more urgent action".

gallery.altText: a close up of king charles, wrinkled and red face with a receding grey hairline
An one-page letter from King Charles to NT residents, acknowledging the devastation of widespread flooding.
Brown water spills across streets in a small community, shown from above.

thumbnail.altText: a close up of king charles, wrinkled and red face with a receding grey hairline
An one-page letter from King Charles to NT residents, acknowledging the devastation of widespread flooding.
Brown water spills across streets in a small community, shown from above.

avatar.alt: A journalist wearing a button-up shirt, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: Post-ceasefire attacks in Lebanon spark fears war would get 'much worse'

children.content: As a shaky ceasefire struggles to get under way, deadly Israeli strikes on Lebanon risk worsening the Middle East conflict.

image.altText: thick black smoke rising from buildings

items.title: Is Hormuz open or closed? Shiptracking data reveals true picture

children.content: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has remained at an effective standstill in the 24 hours since Iran conditionally lifted its blockade on the critical shipping lane, amid a fragile ceasefire with the US and Israel.

image.altText: A satellite image of hundreds of ships floating in the ocean.

items.title: Emperor penguins, Antarctic fur seals now endangered due to climate change

children.content: The iconic emperor penguin and the Antarctic fur seal are edging closer to extinction, according to a projection by the world's largest environmental network.

image.altText: Eleven black and white penguins slide along ice in a row.

gallery.altText: A penguin chicken stands in the foreground. Camera crews are in the distance.

thumbnail.altText: A penguin chicken stands in the foreground. Camera crews are in the distance.

items.title: Singapore's PM knows Australia is vulnerable but Albanese has leverage

children.content: Anthony Albanese is flying to Singapore as part of a hastily arranged regional mission to shore up crucial fuel supplies, amid warnings the global energy crisis could "sharply turn for the worse".

image.altText: Two men shake hands in front of two flags

items.title: China's 26 phone calls that brought on a US-Iran ceasefire

children.content: China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi says he held 26 phone calls with his counterparts in Iran, Israel, Russia and Gulf countries before the ceasefire was reached.

image.altText: A man holds up his index finger while talking.

items.title: Are you in a de facto relationship without realising?

children.content: Our "ordinary understanding” of de facto relationships may not match up with the legal definition and that can cause big surprises for people in the event of a break-up.

articles.title: Comedian Grace Jarvis on most bizarre encounter working in sex shop

children.content: Grace Jarvis talks about everything from her experiences with autism, ADHD, queerness, and working in a sex shop in her stand-up. Her experiences with chronic illness? Not so much.

image.altText: Grace Jarvis smiles while holding a mic and talking on stage.

articles.title: Test your knowledge with our classic Australian TV quiz

children.content: One quote, two possible answers: test your knowledge and take a nostalgic trip down memory lane as you match the quote to the iconic Australian television series.

image.altText: Ray Meagher on the set of Home and Away playing Alf Stewart.

gallery.altText: Back view of a man and woman sitting together on a beach.

thumbnail.altText: Back view of a man and woman sitting together on a beach.

items.title: Man accused of failing to help his dying wife back behind bars

children.content: Kevin Stanischewski is accused of leaving his wife unconscious for hours after she allegedly told him she had drunk poison.

image.altText: A white, modernist, flat-fronted building with "supreme court" in capital letters above a wide entrance.

items.title: Police say 'no evidence of kangaroo strike' in woman's suspicious death

children.content: Northern Territory police say they have been unable to find any evidence of a reported kangaroo strike while investigating a woman's suspicious death on the weekend.

image.altText: A four-wheel-drive with damage to the front of the vehicle

items.title: A wave of low-cost electric trucks is heading for Australia

children.content: Diesel trucks are the backbone of the national supply chain, but figures buried in recent sales data could mark the beginning of the end for these kings of the road.

gallery.altText: An electric prime-mover plugged in to recharge

thumbnail.altText: An electric prime-mover plugged in to recharge

items.title: Uni in financial crisis spent nearly $1.5m on leadership coach, documents show

children.content: Documents released to Four Corners show the University of Technology Sydney spent hundreds of thousands of dollars a year on a leadership coach before planning to slash jobs and courses to save money.

image.altText: A person carrying a bag walks through a university campus. Behind them is a large multi-story glass building.

items.title: Alcoa rejects mercury emissions concerns from its WA refinery

children.content: The US mining giant says increased mercury emissions from its Wagerup refinery are a by-product of higher production, amid a Conservation Council appeal against its operating licence.

image.altText: A large concrete office building with glass windows on a cloudy day, with Alcoa written in large letters.

items.title: 'I've got my visa back': Child sex offender allowed to stay in Australia

children.content: The man's
permanent visa was automatically cancelled when he was jailed in 2024 for sexually abusing a nine-year-old girl, but a tribunal has upheld his appeal in part due to his "ill health".

image.altText: The silhouette of a woman sitting, bending over with head resting on clasped hands

items.title: Mother accused of son's murder told police he would 'shoot up Vegemite'

children.content: Maree Mavis Crabtree is on trial in Brisbane charged with the murder of Jonathan Crabtree in July 2017, and his earlier attempted murder.

image.altText: A woman with sunglasses and wearing a pink and white shirt.

items.title: She hasn't had an injury but $10m horse will never race, heading to retirement instead

children.content: An owner says she has "no regrets" after announcing that the first filly from champion race mare Winx will never race.

gallery.altText: A smiling man with a foal and her mothert, both with dark coats, on a ccountry property.

thumbnail.altText: A smiling man with a foal and her mothert, both with dark coats, on a ccountry property.

avatar.alt: Amelia Bernasconi, wearing a bright pink jacket, leaning on a fence post.

items.title: First glimpse of $600m Sydney Metro West rail designs

children.content: The NSW government says the transport hubs will connect commuters from western areas of Sydney to the CBD in "under 20 minutes".

image.altText: A building with a white angular roof with an 'M' sign with a few trees and gardens around it.

items.title: Mine workers secure permanent $30K pay rise following High Court decision

children.content: The Fair Work Commission ruled last year more than 2,000 mine workers at three BHP coal mines in Queensland were owed the same wages as their full-time counterparts. The High Court has now rejected an attempt to appeal the order, saying it had "insufficient prospects of success".

image.altText: A train wagon filled precisely full with coal

items.title: $1m reward in 2003 cold-case death of Harmony Bryant

children.content: Police hope a $1 million reward will produce fresh information about the death of a 26-year-old Kyogle woman, who was found suffering severe burns in bushland on the NSW Mid North Coast.

image.altText: An image of a young woman, smiling with long brown hair.

items.title: Patient Aussie ousts 'idol' Moore in Bells Beach defence

children.content: Defending champion Isabella Nichols joins Molly Picklum as the last Australians standing at the Bells Beach WSL season-opener.

image.altText: Isabella Nichols sitting on a beach during a surfing competition in Hawaii.

items.title: Top neuroscientists slam AFL move to back new headgear

children.content: The AFL has backed a new helmet that aims to reduce the risk of concussion, but a top neuroscientist is calling it "bad scientific practice".

gallery.altText: five helmets placed on a sporting field
A rugby player wearing a padded black helmet looks to pass the ball.
A black padded sports helmet on a white background.

thumbnail.altText: five helmets placed on a sporting field
A rugby player wearing a padded black helmet looks to pass the ball.
A black padded sports helmet on a white background.

items.title: Woman charged after man allegedly struck with hammer in Sydney hospital

children.content: Viki Graham has been remanded in custody after allegedly attacking a man with a hammer at a Sydney hospital overnight.

image.altText: A close up of Viki Graham with blonde hair and a black top.

items.title: Police call for witnesses after wanted man left with life-threatening injuries

children.content: Police allege the 34-year-old "was seen to be acting suspiciously" near homes at Bray Park near Murwillumbah when he was confronted.

image.altText: A police van and forensic tent set up on the street

items.title: Beloved Queensland pub faces demolition for resort-style development

children.content: The Eimeo Hotel, known for its coastal views and community history, is at risk of being knocked down and locals are worried about the loss of a venue they view as a "Mackay institution".

gallery.altText: A photo of a building with someone holding a historic photo in front.
A building at the top of an outcrop behind a beach.
A building at the top of an outcrop behind a beach.

thumbnail.altText: A photo of a building with someone holding a historic photo in front.
A building at the top of an outcrop behind a beach.
A building at the top of an outcrop behind a beach.
